title: sempervivum arachnoideum subsp. doellianum
intro: Sempervivum arachnoideum subsp. doellianum, also known as the Cobweb Houseleek, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the succulent family (Crassulaceae) and is native to the Canary Islands. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat various health issues such as wounds, ulcers, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sempervivum arachnoideum subsp. doellianum include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
